title: AI Agents Authentication: How Autonomous Systems Prove Identity
snippet: Explores how AI agents authenticate, risks of overly broad scopes, long-lived refresh tokens, and shared tokens across agents without distinct ownership. Notes that authentication decisions are rarely revisited as agents scale.

title: What security headaches has AI introduced in your projects lately? (2026 edition)
snippet: GitHub community discussion on AI-introduced security challenges in 2026, including agent authentication, credential management, and agentic workflow security concerns.

title: OpenAI's Coding Agent Flaw Exposed GitHub Passwords
snippet: BeyondTrust Phantom Labs found a flaw in OpenAI's Codex agent that passed external text into OS commands unchecked, allowing attackers to steal GitHub authentication credentials via hidden instructions in code labels.

title: Hack the AI agent: Build agentic AI security skills with the GitHub Secure Code Game
snippet: GitHub's Secure Code Game challenges developers to build agentic AI security skills, covering prompt injection, credential theft, and authentication bypass in AI agent workflows.

title: Weekly Update – April 20, 2026 | GitHub Agentic Workflows
snippet: GitHub introduced pre-agent-steps frontmatter for running custom Actions steps before AI agents start, enabling authentication setup, environment config, and prerequisite workflows. MCP config moved from .mcp.json to .github/mcp.json.

title: Introducing HUMAN Verified AI Agent: An Open-Source Foundation for Trustworthy Agent Identity
snippet: Open-source demonstration of cryptographically authenticated communication between AI agents and digital services using HTTP Message Signatures (RFC 9421). Agents sign every HTTP request and signatures are verified by a gateway service using a public key registry.

title: Agent Identity (AID) - Authentication & Authorization for AI Agents | Open Source
snippet: Open protocol for AI agent authentication and authorization. Agents prove identity with Ed25519 signatures and receive scoped JWT tokens via OAuth 2.0. MIT licensed, open source.

title: ZeroID: Open-source identity platform for autonomous AI agents
snippet: Open-source identity and credentialing layer for autonomous agents and multi-agent systems. Implements RFC 8693 token exchange to create verifiable delegation chains carrying sub-agent, orchestrator, and authorizing principal identities.

title: Introducing the Agent Governance Toolkit: Open-source runtime security for AI agents
snippet: Microsoft's open-source toolkit including Agent Mesh with cryptographic identity using decentralized identifiers (DIDs) with Ed25519 and the Inter-Agent Trust Protocol (IATP) for secure agent-to-agent communication.

title: Agent Passport – OAuth-like identity verification for AI agents
snippet: Open-source identity verification layer for AI agents described as 'Sign in with Google, but for Agents.' Provides OAuth-like flows adapted for autonomous agent authentication.

title: Securing your agents with authentication and authorization
snippet: LangChain blog post explaining AuthN/AuthZ for agents — OAuth 2.0, OIDC, Auth Code Flow, OBO Token Flow, Client Credentials Flow, and the need for centralized agent auth servers.

title: AI Agent Security & Privacy Guide 2026: Protect Your LangChain Apps
snippet: Comprehensive guide covering security and privacy patterns for LangChain applications in 2026, including agent identity, access control, and data protection.

title: LangChain Tools and Agents 2026: Production-Ready Patterns
snippet: Production-ready patterns for LangChain tools and agents, including authentication integration, permission scoping, and secure tool invocation.

title: March 2026: LangChain Newsletter
snippet: LangChain newsletter covering LangSmith Fleet agent identity, sharing, permissions, and fleet-wide security management features.

title: The best AI agent frameworks in 2026
snippet: Comparison of AI agent frameworks in 2026 covering authentication, authorization, multi-service spanning, and audit challenges unique to autonomous agents.

title: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Protocol - CrewAI
snippet: CrewAI treats A2A as a first-class delegation primitive, enabling agents to delegate tasks, request information, and collaborate with remote agents. Supports HTTPBasicAuth and token-based authentication for secure inter-agent communication.

title: feat: implement hierarchical agent delegation with allowed_agents parameter
snippet: Introduces controlled hierarchical agent structures through the allowed_agents parameter, enabling agents to only delegate to specified subordinates and reducing choice paralysis in multi-agent crews.

title: Hierarchical AI Agents: A Guide to CrewAI Delegation
snippet: Guide to CrewAI's hierarchical delegation model where manager agents coordinate specialist agents, covering allow_delegation flags, responsibility boundaries, and controlled task routing.

title: [Feature] Trust-verified task delegation with Agent Governance Toolkit
snippet: Proposes a governance callback/middleware for CrewAI to enforce organizational policies before agent task delegation — controlling which agents can delegate to whom, what data can flow between tasks, and whether human approval is required.

title: [FEATURE] Allow delegation to specific agents only
snippet: Feature request to restrict delegation targets so agents can only delegate to a defined subset of other agents, preventing uncontrolled lateral delegation in large multi-agent crews.

title: Agent Identity and Lifecycle — AutoGen
snippet: Agent ID uniquely identifies an agent instance within an agent runtime and serves as the address for receiving messages. Agent types are defined in application code; agent keys are generated from messages delivered to agents.

title: Agentic Identity and Access Management (AIAM) · microsoft/autogen · Discussion #5921
snippet: AIAM provides each agent with a unique, verifiable identity; enforces fine-grained access permissions; and ensures all actions are auditable. Separates four identities: human principal, agent runtime identity, delegation identity, and tool credential.

title: Zero-Trust Agents: Adding Identity and Access to Multi-Agent Workflows | Microsoft Community Hub
snippet: A zero-trust approach to autonomous AI agents integrating identity and access management into enterprise agentic workflows, where each AI agent has identity and issued access token for every action.

title: Authentication and Authorization · microsoft/autogen · Discussion #4656
snippet: Discussion on authentication methods for AutoGen agents including API keys, Azure Active Directory token credentials, and certificate-based authentication for agent-runtime communication.
